title: “Risks” and “communication”: conceptual discussion from the perspective of complex transdisciplinarity
content: This article explores the concept of risk communication, highlighting the lack of a unified definition and proposing a transdisciplinary, complex-thinking approach to better understand it.
snippet: This article explores the concept of risk communication, highlighting the lack of a unified definition and proposing a transdisciplinary, complex-thinking approach to better understand it.

title: Leveraging community-driven development platforms for resilience: lessons from a global knowledge exchange in Jakarta
content: Nearby in Margagiri, villages have combined micro dams, drainage improvements, and agroforestry into an integrated water and land management system that has reduced damage from heavy rains and improved agricultural production.

title: Here are 6 ways that climate change is affecting sports around the world
content: The sports economy is worth $2.3 trillion a year, but faces challenges from our changing climate. A new World Economic Forum report outlines pathways to ensure resilience.
snippet: The sports economy is worth $2.3 trillion a year, but faces challenges from our changing climate. A new World Economic Forum report outlines pathways to ensure resilience.

title: Understanding how plants pause and restart growth can help develop climate‑resilient crops
content: When plants face biting cold, floods or parched soil, they can’t run away or seek shelter like animals. Instead, they have to develop ways to overcome and survive them until the weather improves.
snippet: When plants face biting cold, floods or parched soil, they can’t run away or seek shelter like animals. Instead, they have to develop ways to overcome and survive them until the weather improves.

title: The science behind the headlines: understanding attribution science
content: Attribution science has emerged over the past few decades to answer precisely these questions. And like any scientific field, it deserves thoughtful examination of its methods, limitations, and contributions.

title: Advancing climate services for health: new milestones from the WHO–WMO Accelerator
content: Transforming climate science into meaningful health protection requires sustained collaboration and capacity building across sectors, and services that are robust and long-lasting.
snippet: Transforming climate science into meaningful health protection requires sustained collaboration and capacity building across sectors, and services that are robust and long-lasting.

title: Building resilience or building risk? Lessons from Sapatana Ashrayan in Bangladesh
content: As part of the CDKN Knowledge Accelerator, a team from alliance partners, ICLEI South Asia, visited Ward 9 of Lalmonirhat Municipality in Bangladesh. The visit focused on assessing the Sapatana Ashrayan national housing project.

title: Toxins plus climate harms likely cause of reduced fertility, study finds
content: Researchers find ‘alarming’ effect on fertility across global species from simultaneous exposures
snippet: Researchers find ‘alarming’ effect on fertility across global species from simultaneous exposures

title: Primary disruptions of extreme storms and floods on critical entities under the framework of the CER EU Directive: the case of storm Daniel in Greece
content: This work examines the impacts of extreme storms and subsequent flooding on critical entities as defined under the new EU Directive (Critical Entities Resilience, CER).
snippet: This work examines the impacts of extreme storms and subsequent flooding on critical entities as defined under the new EU Directive (Critical Entities Resilience, CER).

title: The next El Niño could lock Earth into a hotter climate
content: The Pacific Ocean is a giant climate cauldron, with a powerful heat engine that affects storms, fisheries and rainfall patterns half a world away, and scientists are watching closely to see if it’s about to boil over.

title: Global climate surge report 2024-2025: climate crisis & Pakistan's vulnerability
content: The Global climate surge report 2024–2025 presents a comprehensive assessment of accelerating global climate trends and their implications for Pakistan.
snippet: The Global climate surge report 2024–2025 presents a comprehensive assessment of accelerating global climate trends and their implications for Pakistan.

content: This policy analysis highlights the need for stronger integration and defines synergies as the intentional coordination of planning and implementation across NDCs, NAPs, and NBSAPs to achieve more effective outcomes and co-benefits.

title: Vice-rectorat de Polynésie française
content: To implement this constitutional requirement for the benefit of Polynesian children, adolescents, and young adults, the Ministry of National Education established the services of the Vice-Rectorate of French Polynesia on January 15, 1970.

title: Heat and pregnancy: guidance for healthcare professionals and community health workers
content: The World Health Organization has recently published a conceptual framework on hashtag#maternal health, highlighting the importance of mitigating heat-related risks.
snippet: The World Health Organization has recently published a conceptual framework on hashtag#maternal health, highlighting the importance of mitigating heat-related risks.

title: Urban heat governance: assessing institutional capacity to manage extreme heat in cities of the Global South
content: This policy brief presents findings from an analysis of heat-related urban governance across 83 coastal cities in the Global South.
snippet: This policy brief presents findings from an analysis of heat-related urban governance across 83 coastal cities in the Global South.

title: Rising temperatures, silent suffering: lived experiences of heat stress and its impact on health among street vendors
content: Extreme heat is a growing public health concern that disproportionately affects certain disadvantaged communities. The situation calls for urgent action—placing adaptation and resilience-building on par with mitigation efforts.

title: Developing nature-based solution pathways to new layers of complexities for urban communities coping with armed conflict and climate change
content: This study was conducted to scientifically develop a pathway to urban climate resilience by exploring pragmatic approaches from communities and local humanitarian organizations experiencing the compounding risks of armed conflict and climate change.

title: Rethinking how India adapts to extreme heat
content: A new white paper from the Salata Institute's Climate Adaptation in South Asia research cluster brings together perspectives to examine how extreme heat is reshaping life and livelihoods across India.
snippet: A new white paper from the Salata Institute's Climate Adaptation in South Asia research cluster brings together perspectives to examine how extreme heat is reshaping life and livelihoods across India.

title: Bridging the gaps: vulnerability profiles to strengthen impactbased early warning and early action for floods and droughts in Kenya
content: This report addresses a critical gap in disaster preparedness in Kenya: early warning systems for floods and droughts that are tailored to the specific needs of the most vulnerable population groups.
snippet: This report addresses a critical gap in disaster preparedness in Kenya: early warning systems for floods and droughts that are tailored to the specific needs of the most vulnerable population groups.

title: Investigating and scaling-up effective nature-based solutions for climate resilience in Dessie City administration, Ethiopia
content: This study opted to investigate and scale up effective nature-based solutions (NbS) for climate resilience in Dessie City, Ethiopia, using a mixed research approach.
snippet: This study opted to investigate and scale up effective nature-based solutions (NbS) for climate resilience in Dessie City, Ethiopia, using a mixed research approach.

title: Edible rooftop gardens: a community and nature-based climate resilience building strategy
content: This study investigates the heat adaptation potential of edible rooftop gardens in Chennai, India, through a mixed-methods approach combining quantitative monitoring and qualitative analysis.
snippet: This study investigates the heat adaptation potential of edible rooftop gardens in Chennai, India, through a mixed-methods approach combining quantitative monitoring and qualitative analysis.

title: Councils want government to pay for more for natural hazard protections
content: Councils are calling on the government to foot more of the bill for improving New Zealand's disaster resilience in the wake of last week's devastating storms.
snippet: Councils are calling on the government to foot more of the bill for improving New Zealand's disaster resilience in the wake of last week's devastating storms.

snippet: This case study focuses on how the protection of persons in the event of disasters (PPED) treaty could amplify the efforts of the Lebanese government to enhance disaster risk management

title: How investors can bridge the funding gap for resilient infrastructure
content: Traditional infrastructure-funding models rarely factor in resilience to geoclimatic events. Yet, the impact of extreme weather on existing and future global infrastructure could generate economic losses as high as 19% of GDP by 2050.

title: Stronger together: Fiji and the case for a global disaster treaty
content: This case study narrates Fiji's journey in disaster law and governance.
snippet: This case study narrates Fiji's journey in disaster law and governance.

title: The 2026 Europe report of the Lancet Countdown on health and climate change: narrowing window for decisive health action
content: The report features seven new indicators, methodological updates, extended time series for existing indicators, and highlights inequalities in health risks and impacts where possible.
snippet: The report features seven new indicators, methodological updates, extended time series for existing indicators, and highlights inequalities in health risks and impacts where possible.

title: From cyclones to cooperation: the case for a disaster treaty in Madagascar
content: This publication explores how recurring cyclones and other climate-related hazards are intensifying risks in Madagascar, causing widespread damage to infrastructure, livelihoods, and food security.
snippet: This publication explores how recurring cyclones and other climate-related hazards are intensifying risks in Madagascar, causing widespread damage to infrastructure, livelihoods, and food security.

title: Third GEM-EGU Summer School on GeoHazards Risk Assessment

content: This study develops a multidimensional framework integrating vulnerability, susceptibility, and adaptability assessments to evaluate regional flood emergency capacity.
snippet: This study develops a multidimensional framework integrating vulnerability, susceptibility, and adaptability assessments to evaluate regional flood emergency capacity.

title: 2026 Global report on food crises
content: The Global Report on Food Crises (GRFC) 2026 reveals that acute food insecurity and malnutrition remain at alarmingly high and deeply entrenched levels, with crises increasingly concentrated in a core group of countries.

title: Human-altered estuaries now drive stronger tides farther inland
content: A study led by Wageningen University & Research shows that human interventions have significantly changed tides in river estuaries over the past centuries.
snippet: A study led by Wageningen University & Research shows that human interventions have significantly changed tides in river estuaries over the past centuries.

title: Why delaying climate action now means higher seas by 2100
content: Rising global temperatures are accelerating ice melt and ocean expansion, making significant sea-level rise increasingly unavoidable and threatening coastlines worldwide.
snippet: Rising global temperatures are accelerating ice melt and ocean expansion, making significant sea-level rise increasingly unavoidable and threatening coastlines worldwide.

title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
content: An early flood warning system designed to save the lives of thousands of people in the Everest region may no longer be working, Nepalese officials have admitted to the BBC, after it was allowed to fall into a state of disrepair.

title: The unusual ways Fijians predict when a cyclone is approaching
content: In 2024, the Fijian Meteorological Service announced it would integrate traditional environmental knowledge into its scientific forecasting – describing the pair as "a total package".
snippet: In 2024, the Fijian Meteorological Service announced it would integrate traditional environmental knowledge into its scientific forecasting – describing the pair as "a total package".

title: A survival guide for assessing global fire risks to natural and human systems
content: This paper provides an overview of the systemic pathways of fire impacts, available remote-sensing-derived fire products and modelling efforts.
snippet: This paper provides an overview of the systemic pathways of fire impacts, available remote-sensing-derived fire products and modelling efforts.

title: Reformative recovery for resilience in disaster risk management: A scoping review and analytical framework

title: Toward disaster-resilient universities: A pre-COVID assessment of American institutions of higher education
content: This article investigates the state of resilience of academic institutions in the Southeastern United States prior to the COVID-19 pandemic.
snippet: This article investigates the state of resilience of academic institutions in the Southeastern United States prior to the COVID-19 pandemic.
